run.engine_proxy: isolated-subagent workflow (curator ≠ blind-engine ≠ scorer, separation of duties; )

reference_claims.text: The project is a monorepo managed with Yarn workspaces.
packages/excalidraw/ exists as the main React component library.
packages/excalidraw/ is published to npm as @excalidraw/excalidraw.
excalidraw-app/ exists as the full-featured web application (excalidraw.com).
examples/ contains integration examples: a NextJS example and a browser-script example.
Internal packages are referenced via path aliases configured in vitest.config.mts.
The package build system uses esbuild.
The web application is built with Vite.
The codebase is TypeScript throughout with strict configuration enabled.

emitted_claims.claim: The repository is a Yarn-workspaces monorepo named excalidraw-monorepo; workspaces are excalidraw-app, packages/*, and examples/*.
@excalidraw/common is a foundational package (31 files, 182 exports) holding shared constants, color palettes, key/event helpers, math-free utility types and primitives.
@excalidraw/element is the element-model package (77 files, 334 exports) defining all ExcalidrawElement types and element operations (binding, resize, frames, arrows, fractional indexing).
@excalidraw/math is a geometry primitives package (26 files, 74 exports: points, vectors, curves, ellipses, line-segments).
@excalidraw/utils is a thin cross-cutting utilities package (12 files, 11 exports) including export-to-canvas/SVG helpers and GeometricShape.
@excalidraw/excalidraw is the main React editor library package (418 files, 97 public exports) published to npm; it contains the App component, actions, renderer, scene, data, and i18n.
@excalidraw/fractional-indexing is a tiny leaf package (4 files, 3 exports: generateKeyBetween, generateNKeysBetween, validateOrderKey) with no internal package dependencies.
excalidraw-app is the full web application (excalidraw.com) that consumes the @excalidraw/excalidraw library and adds collaboration, persistence, and sharing.
The App class (extends React.Component) is the central editor orchestrator: it instantiates and owns the Scene, Store, and History and exposes the ExcalidrawImperativeAPI.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:620 (class App extends React.Component); App.tsx:826 (this.scene = new Scene()), :833 (this.store = new Store(this)), :834 (this.history = new History(this.store))

emitted_claims.claim: Scene is the in-memory authoritative element store: it holds the canonical elementsMap and nonDeletedElementsMap (branded Maps) and serves element queries to the rest of the editor.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/element/src/Scene.ts:108 (class Scene); Scene.ts:117 (nonDeletedElementsMap), :125 (private elementsMap = toBrandedType<SceneElementsMap>(new Map()))

emitted_claims.claim: Store captures observed scene/appstate changes and emits them as StoreIncrement events (durable vs ephemeral), feeding undo/redo and collaboration.
History implements undo/redo via two stacks (undoStack, redoStack) of HistoryDelta entries fed by the Store.
Collab (Portal) is the real-time collaboration transport: it manages the socket.io connection, room id/key, and broadcasts element updates to a collaboration room.
Collaboration scene state and files are persisted to Google Firebase ג€” Firestore for scene documents and Firebase Storage for files.
Local persistence uses the browser: localStorage for scene/appState (with quota-exceeded handling) and IndexedDB (via idb-keyval) for binary files in a "files-db"/"files-store".
Application state is managed with Jotai atoms (collabAPIAtom, isCollaboratingAtom, localStorageQuotaExceededAtom) via a dedicated appJotaiStore.
Rendering uses RoughJS for hand-drawn shapes and React for UI; the app is built with Vite, the packages with esbuild (per build scripts).
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:5 (import rough from "roughjs/bin/rough"); excalidraw-app/vite.config.mts:88 (outDir:"build"); package.json (build:app vs build:packages scripts)

emitted_claims.claim: The web app bootstraps in excalidraw-app/index.tsx: it creates a React root on #root, registers a service worker (PWA), and renders <ExcalidrawApp/> in StrictMode.
The library's public API entry is packages/excalidraw/index.tsx, exporting the <Excalidraw> component, ExcalidrawAPIProvider, and the ExcalidrawImperativeAPI surface for host applications.

Element identity for collaboration is the (version, versionNonce) pair: every mutation bumps element.version (+1), regenerates a random versionNonce, and sets the updated timestamp ג€” these MUST advance monotonically or remote-merge will be wrong.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/element/src/mutateElement.ts:139-141 (element.version = updates.version ?? element.version+1; versionNonce = randomInteger(); updated = getUpdatedTimestamp()); mutateElement.ts:185 (bumpVersion)

emitted_claims.claim: Concurrent-edit conflicts are resolved deterministically: a remote element is discarded when local is being edited, OR local.version > remote.version, OR (equal versions) local.versionNonce <= remote.versionNonce (lowest-nonce-wins tiebreak). Changing this tiebreak breaks convergence.

Fractional indices must stay in sync with array order and be strictly ordered between predecessor and successor; a violation throws InvalidFractionalIndexError (in dev/test/DEBUG), and bound text must have a smaller index than its container.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/element/src/fractionalIndex.ts:74 ("Fractional indices invariant has been compromised"); fractionalIndex.ts:89 (bound-elements invariant); fractionalIndex.ts:44 (@throws InvalidFractionalIndexError)

emitted_claims.claim: Undoability is governed by CaptureUpdateAction with exactly three modes ג€” IMMEDIATELY (local edits, captured to undo stack), NEVER (remote/init updates, never captured), EVENTUALLY (deferred capture). Mis-tagging an update corrupts undo/redo and collab.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/element/src/store.ts:38-69 (CaptureUpdateAction = { IMMEDIATELY, NEVER, EVENTUALLY } as const, with the NEVER doc "remote updates or scene initialization")

emitted_claims.claim: Embeddable iframe URLs must be validated by the trusted editor before rendering; the embedsValidationStatus flag is intentionally NOT reset on URL change so validation provenance (trusted source) is guaranteed.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:652-658 (embedsValidationStatus comment: "guarantee the validation came from a trusted source (the editor)")

emitted_claims.claim: Collaboration data is end-to-end encrypted: the Portal encrypts payloads with encryptData before broadcasting, using AES-GCM (Web Crypto subtle) with a 12-byte IV; the room key never leaves the client unencrypted.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: excalidraw-app/collab/Portal.tsx:3 (import encryptData from data/encryption); packages/excalidraw/data/encryption.ts:17-23 (AES-GCM generateKey, encrypt/decrypt), :5 (IV_LENGTH_BYTES=12)

emitted_claims.claim: The dependency direction is one-way at the app boundary: packages/* never import from excalidraw-app (the app consumes the library, not vice-versa); the only such import is in a package test-util, not in shipped code.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/excalidraw/tests/test-utils.ts (sole file importing excalidraw-app; verified via grep over packages/ ג€” no non-test source imports excalidraw-app); excalidraw-app/App.tsx:2 (imports from @excalidraw/excalidraw)

emitted_claims.claim: common is the lowest layer (imported by element, excalidraw, math, utils) and must not depend on app-level concerns; the internal package graph is intentionally cyclic among common/element/excalidraw/math/utils (mutual type imports), so adding cross-package value-imports risks bundling cycles.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: structural-graph.json (common.imported_by=[element,excalidraw,math,utils]; element.imports_from includes excalidraw & vice-versa ג€” mutual cycle)

emitted_claims.claim: Fractional index z-order ordering breaks ties deterministically: identical fractional indices fall back to element id ordering, and missing indices preserve array order ג€” required for stable cross-client z-order.
emitted_claims.code_anchor: packages/element/src/fractionalIndex.ts:129-131 (orderByFractionalIndex: "when fractional indices are identical, break the tie based on the element id")

emitted_claims.claim: The release/build git SHA is injected at build time and exposed on window.__EXCALIDRAW_SHA__ from the Vite env (VITE_APP_GIT_SHA).
Firebase configuration is supplied via the VITE_APP_FIREBASE_CONFIG env var (JSON-parsed at runtime), so the app is configured for self-hosting via environment, not hard-coded credentials.

adjudication.note: EC-01 captures the Yarn-workspaces monorepo with the exact workspace globs (excalidraw-app, packages/*, examples/*).
EC-06 identifies @excalidraw/excalidraw as the main React editor library package.
Fact-keyed: EC-06 explicitly states the package is 'published to npm' as @excalidraw/excalidraw, capturing the publish technology-choice despite the component-exists label.
EC-08 describes excalidraw-app as the full web application (excalidraw.com).
Fact-keyed: EC-08 states the app consumes/imports @excalidraw/excalidraw (also EC-26 confirms the app->library edge).
EC-02 identifies @excalidraw/common as a foundational package.
EC-03 identifies @excalidraw/element as the element-model package.
EC-04 identifies @excalidraw/math as the geometry primitives package.
EC-05 identifies @excalidraw/utils as the thin cross-cutting utilities package.

No EC asserts the examples/ integration examples (NextJS + browser-script) as a component. EC-01 only lists examples/* as a workspace glob, not the existence of the two named example components. Blind run provably read package.json workspaces, so the examples/ referent was readable.

No EC captures the path-alias resolution mechanism (vitest.config.mts resolve.alias / tsconfig paths mapping @excalidraw/* to packages/*/src). EC-26/EC-27 cover dependency direction and graph cyclicity, a different fact. vitest.config.mts/tsconfig.json were readable by the blind run.
EC-17 states 'the packages with esbuild (per build scripts).'
EC-17 states 'the app is built with Vite' (also EC-18/EC-29/EC-30 cite vite config).

No EC asserts 'TypeScript throughout with strict config enabled' as a technology-choice. The language is implied by pervasive .ts/.tsx anchors but the distinctive load-bearing fact (tsconfig strict:true) is never stated. tsconfig.json was readable by the blind run.
